triggers.label: German corporate restructuring mass layoff profits 2005
triggers.description: Profits from mass layoffs disguised as efficiency gains; cynical labor market terminology

triggers.label: rise of broadband streaming media services
triggers.description: Broadband internet and services such as YouTube (2005), Netflix streaming (2007) and Spotify (2008) made consuming audio and video as a continuous online flow mainstream, fixing the verb 'to stream' in the media sense.

triggers.label: Amazon Web Services launch and popularisation of cloud computing (2006)
triggers.description: Amazon's launch of AWS EC2 and S3 in 2006, followed by Eric Schmidt's use of 'cloud computing' at the 2006 Search Engine Strategies conference, anchored the term in mainstream technology discourse.

triggers.label: 2006 FIFA World Cup public viewing Germany
triggers.description: Connected to public viewing culture during the 2006 FIFA World Cup in Germany

triggers.label: German asylum deportation policy coerced departure euphemism
triggers.description: Asylum seekers under deportation threat have no genuine choice; cynical official bureaucratic language

triggers.label: Launch of Twitter social media platform (2006)
triggers.description: Twitter launched in 2006 with a deliberate bird metaphor; users organically adopted 'tweet' for a post by 2007, which Twitter later formalised in its UI.

triggers.label: German childcare home care benefit political debate 2007
triggers.description: Pejorative term disparaging childcare benefit for stay-at-home parents as 'stove bonus'

triggers.label: IPCC Fourth Assessment Report climate crisis 2007
triggers.description: Addressed consequences of uncontrolled global warming amid IPCC reports and climate debate

triggers.label: Lehman Brothers collapse global financial crisis 2008
triggers.description: Named the global banking and financial crisis triggered by the collapse of Lehman Brothers

triggers.label: youth party culture over-30 parties generational slang
triggers.description: Youth slang for Ü30 parties (over-30 parties); portmanteau treating older party-goers as spoiled meat

triggers.label: 2008 global financial crisis bank bailout rhetoric
triggers.description: Reverses causality in financial crisis: banks described as 'suffering' despite causing the crisis

triggers.label: Facebook 'friend' / 'unfriend' interface (mid-2000s)
triggers.description: Facebook's friends model, opened to the general public in 2006, gave 'friend' and 'unfriend' precise platform meanings; 'unfriend' was named New Oxford American Dictionary Word of the Year in 2009.

triggers.label: Facebook Like button launch (2009)
triggers.description: Facebook launched its 'Like' button in February 2009; the feature spread across the web and social platforms, turning the everyday verb into a one-click social endorsement and a countable noun ('likes').

triggers.label: German economic stimulus cash for clunkers program 2009
triggers.description: Described cash-for-clunkers vehicle scrapping incentive program as economic stimulus

triggers.label: German employer anti-union works council rhetoric 2009
triggers.description: Trade union representative described workers' councils as 'contaminated with works council disease'

triggers.label: Hartz IV unemployment welfare stigma youth slang
triggers.description: Derived from Hartz IV welfare reform; means to be unemployed, hang around; Hartz IV as cultural stigma

triggers.label: Angela Merkel's repeated use of 'alternativlos' 2009-2010
triggers.description: Chancellor Merkel systematically used 'alternativlos' to justify the Euro rescue package, health reform, and Stuttgart 21, prompting the Gesellschaft fuer deutsche Sprache to name it Unwort des Jahres 2010.

triggers.label: Lil B's reappropriation of 'based' (c. 2010)
triggers.description: Around 2010 the rapper Lil B redefined 'based', previously a drug-related slur, to mean being positive and true to oneself; internet communities then spread it as a term of approval.

triggers.label: Merkel austerity policy alternativlos rhetoric 2010
triggers.description: Merkel's claim that austerity policy decisions had no alternatives; falsely forecloses political debate

triggers.label: WikiLeaks Julian Assange secret information leaks
triggers.description: Anonymous publication of secret information via internet; WikiLeaks made the term mainstream

triggers.label: youth party culture declining standards social commentary
triggers.description: Portmanteau of Niveau (level) and limbo dance; steady decline of standards, especially at parties

triggers.label: Stuttgart 21 railway protest movement Germany 2010
triggers.description: Reflected emerging angry citizen protest culture, coined during Stuttgart 21 railway protests

triggers.label: Spiegel essay 'Der Wutbuerger' by Dirk Kurbjuweit
triggers.description: The essay in Der Spiegel Issue 41 of 11 October 2010 coined the term and made it immediately prominent, tying it to Stuttgart 21 protests.

triggers.label: Social-media meme culture (2010s, r/cringe)
triggers.description: Merriam-Webster records the slang adjective from 1983. Online meme and reaction culture, including the r/cringe subreddit (founded 2011) and the 'cringe compilation' video genre, spread the predicative adjective/noun use widely in the 2010s but did not coin it.

triggers.label: Eli Pariser, 'The Filter Bubble' (2011)
triggers.description: Eli Pariser's 2011 book 'The Filter Bubble' coined the concept; the German loan-translation 'Filterblase' became the standard term for algorithmic information isolation in German media debate.

triggers.label: NSU neo-Nazi murder series Turkish German victims
triggers.description: Reduces NSU neo-Nazi hate crimes murdering Turkish-German business owners to food metaphor

triggers.label: social media outrage culture Twitter Facebook rise Germany
triggers.description: Wave of online outrage in social networks; entered German public and even official discourse

triggers.label: Fukushima nuclear disaster bank stress tests 2011
triggers.description: Referenced extensive testing of nuclear power plants and banks after Fukushima disaster

triggers.label: hip-hop culture swag concept German youth language adoption
triggers.description: Borrowed from hip-hop culture; enviously admired cool, stylish charisma and attitude

triggers.label: Established as mainstream German internet discourse term
Kickstarter internet crowdfunding startup culture rise
triggers.description: Raising capital from many small individual contributions via internet; new startup and creative funding model

triggers.label: German misogynist victim-blaming discourse sexual violence
triggers.description: Accuses women of holding a subscription to victimhood; trivializes sexual violence claims

triggers.label: European sovereign debt crisis bailout packages
triggers.description: Described regular government emergency financial rescue packages during Euro debt crisis

triggers.label: Drake YOLO hip-hop internet meme spread 2012
triggers.description: Acronym 'you only live once'; internet/Drake hip-hop phrase urging to seize opportunities

triggers.label: Black Lives Matter movement
triggers.description: The BLM movement, especially after 2013, mainstreamed 'woke' as a marker of social-justice awareness.

triggers.label: Haftbefehl Babo rap song German youth slang spread 2013
triggers.description: Turkish/Kurdish origin word for boss, leader; spread through German hip-hop especially Haftbefehl song

triggers.label: NSA surveillance scandal Germany Watergate suffix spread
triggers.description: Suffix for designating scandals; derived from Watergate; NSA-Gate and other scandals spread the suffix

triggers.label: CDU SPD grand coalition negotiations Germany 2013
triggers.description: Abbreviated 'Große Koalition'; CDU/CSU-SPD grand coalition negotiations after federal election

triggers.label: EU free movement benefit tourism debate Germany 2013
triggers.description: Stigmatizes Eastern European EU migrants as benefit tourists; contested by research showing opposite

triggers.label: racial sensitivity debate blackface Germany theater carnival
triggers.description: White actors painted black in theatrical/carnival contexts; racial sensitivity debate reached Germany

triggers.label: German internet meme ironic praise social media spread
triggers.description: Ironic internet meme phrase meaning 'things are going well for you'; spread through social media

triggers.label: 25th anniversary Berlin Wall fall light installation 2014
triggers.description: Referenced the light installation for the 25th anniversary of the Fall of the Berlin Wall

triggers.label: Pegida movement right-wing press delegitimization 2014
triggers.description: Nazi-era attack on press freedom revived by Pegida and right-wing movements; echoes Goebbels propaganda

triggers.label: PEGIDA movement demonstrations, Dresden 2014
triggers.description: From October 2014, PEGIDA demonstrators chanted 'Luegenpresse' at journalists, reactivating a Nazi-era slur and cementing its association with the far-right. Unwort des Jahres 2014.

triggers.label: German gender-inclusive language debate (2010s)
triggers.description: The 2010s German debate over gender-inclusive language popularised the asterisk form (Leser*innen); DWDS records the inclusive sense and a frequency rise across the decade (corpus examples 2009-2018).

triggers.label: European migration crisis Germany 2015
triggers.description: Contextualized mass migration of refugees from Middle East and Africa to Europe and Germany

triggers.label: refugee crisis Germany right-wing backlash humanitarian critics 2015
triggers.description: Ridicules refugee advocates as naive do-gooders; used to undermine compassion during refugee crisis

triggers.label: European refugee crisis Germany welcome culture 2015
triggers.description: English slogan for welcome culture during refugee crisis; used in German protests and media

triggers.label: smartphone addiction distracted pedestrian culture youth
triggers.description: Portmanteau of smartphone and zombie; people distracted by devices; spread through mobile phone culture

triggers.label: Mainstreaming of mobile dating apps (Tinder, 2012-)
triggers.description: Swipe-based mobile dating apps such as Tinder (launched 2012) normalised low-friction matching and easy disappearance, and the term 'ghosting' for abrupt no-explanation withdrawal spread rapidly in 2015.

triggers.label: Gutmensch chosen as Unwort des Jahres 2015
triggers.description: In January 2016 a language-critical jury named Gutmensch the non-word of 2015, reflecting how the term had become a right-wing polemical weapon to dismiss liberal opponents.

triggers.label: Rise of Instagram and influencer marketing (mid-2010s)
triggers.description: The growth of Instagram (launched 2010) and the surrounding creator economy turned 'influencer' into a job title for paid social-media promoters, narrowing the generic sense to this commercial meaning by the mid-2010s.

triggers.label: Political and media discourse surge (2016-2018)
triggers.description: From the 2016 US election onward, commentators applied 'gaslighting' to political messaging and misinformation, broadening it from a clinical term to a general label for deceptive manipulation; Merriam-Webster reported a steep lookup and usage rise.

triggers.label: Trump election Brexit deliberate disinformation fake news 2016
triggers.description: Deliberately false information spread as news; Trump election and Brexit made term ubiquitous

triggers.label: hip-hop fly aesthetic German youth language adoption
triggers.description: From hip-hop culture meaning to be attractive, stylish, great; someone who stands out positively

triggers.label: Brexit Trump post-truth politics rise 2016
triggers.description: Indicated emotion-driven post-truth discourse replacing fact-based debate; rise of populism and Brexit/Trump

triggers.label: German far-right populist anti-politician rhetoric 2016
triggers.description: Nazi-era term meaning 'traitor of the people' revived to attack democratically elected politicians

triggers.label: Social-media call-out culture and #MeToo (2017)
triggers.description: The 2017 surge of social-media accountability movements, especially #MeToo, popularised 'cancelling' public figures, fixing the new transitive sense of 'to cancel someone'.

triggers.label: Kellyanne Conway alternative facts Trump inauguration 2017
triggers.description: Kellyanne Conway phrase legitimizing deliberate falsehoods in public discourse; post-truth politics

triggers.label: Vong-Sprache German internet meme absurdist language 2017
triggers.description: Internet meme dialect phrase meaning 'I am' in Vong-Sprache; viral absurdist internet language

triggers.label: social media influencer marketing YouTube Instagram rise
triggers.description: Online celebrities who exert influence on their audience; social media marketing rose to prominence

triggers.label: failed Jamaica coalition negotiations Germany 2017
triggers.description: Marked the failure of Jamaica coalition negotiations (CDU/CSU-FDP-Greens) after federal election

triggers.label: German asylum legal aid NGO vilification 2018
triggers.description: Mischaracterizes asylum legal support and NGOs as profit-driven deportation-prevention industry

triggers.label: hip-hop honor culture German youth praise slang
triggers.description: Person of honor who does something particularly good or kind; spreading from hip-hop culture

triggers.label: German gender-inclusive language reform feminist activism
triggers.description: Gender star (*) for gender-inclusive writing; feminist linguistic activism in German language

triggers.label: European extreme heat summer 2018 climate change
triggers.description: Described the extreme heat summer of 2018 and climate change; wordplay on 'Eiszeit' (ice age)

triggers.label: Greta Thunberg climate activism and FridaysForFuture
US culture-war polarisation (2019-)
triggers.description: Conservative politicians and media adopted 'woke' as a derisive label for progressive excess, completing the reversal.

triggers.label: Fridays for Future Greta Thunberg climate movement 2019
triggers.description: Phraseological formula from Fridays for Future climate movement; applied to other activist contexts

triggers.label: Fridays for Future climate movement delegitimization 2019
triggers.description: Delegitimizes climate protection efforts and Fridays for Future activists as hysterical

triggers.label: German minimum pension reform proposal 2019
triggers.description: Represented newly-coined political term for minimum pension reform proposal

triggers.label: COVID-19 pandemic
triggers.description: The 2020 COVID-19 pandemic, with its constant stream of alarming news, drove an explosion in the use of 'doomscrolling'; Merriam-Webster added it to its watch list and dictionary in 2020.

triggers.label: COVID-19 pandemic measures Querdenken conspiracy movement 2020
triggers.description: Used by extremists to delegitimize democratic pandemic health measures as dictatorship

triggers.label: COVID-19 coronavirus global pandemic 2020
triggers.description: Named the dominant topic of nearly the entire year; COVID-19 pandemic

triggers.label: COVID-19 pandemic lockdown restrictions Germany 2020
triggers.description: Pandemic restriction measures including contact bans; COVID-19 made the English term standard in German

triggers.label: gaming social media youth slang digital communication
triggers.description: Anglicism meaning clueless, lost, confused; spread through social media and gaming culture

triggers.label: EU migrant deportation policy euphemism 2020
triggers.description: Cynically euphemizes EU migration deportation policy as voluntary sponsorship

triggers.label: COVID-19 vaccination debate (2020-2021)
triggers.description: The 2020-2021 public debate over COVID-19 vaccination sharply politicised 'Impfgegner', loading the descriptive term with a derogatory, conflict-laden charge; DWDS shows a steep frequency rise.

triggers.label: Querdenken 711 protest movement
triggers.description: Anti-lockdown protest movement founded in Stuttgart in 2020, which fixed the new pejorative reading of the word.

triggers.label: COVID-19 pandemic and Querdenken protests in Germany
triggers.description: The COVID-19 pandemic and associated anti-vaccination and lockdown-sceptic movements, including Querdenken, drove the narrowing of Schwurbler to a specifically political pejorative label for conspiracy theorists.

triggers.label: Among Us popularity surge (2020)
triggers.description: The social-deduction game Among Us, released in 2018, exploded in popularity during the 2020 lockdowns; players accusing each other of being 'sus' (suspicious) propelled the clipped form into mainstream internet slang.

triggers.label: COVID-19 booster vaccination campaign Germany 2021
triggers.description: To receive a COVID-19 booster vaccination; Germanized verb from English 'boost' during pandemic

triggers.label: YouTube streaming culture youth embarrassment slang
triggers.description: Anglicism describing embarrassing or second-hand shame situations; spread through YouTube and streaming

triggers.label: EU border pushback illegal migration human rights violations 2021
triggers.description: Military-bureaucratic term obscuring human rights violations: illegal pushbacks of migrants at EU borders

triggers.label: fourth COVID wave lockdown measures Germany 2021
triggers.description: Represented measures taken to break the 4th corona wave; lockdown metaphor

triggers.label: Letzte Generation climate activists criminalization Germany 2022
triggers.description: Criminalizes nonviolent climate activist groups such as Letzte Generation as terrorists

triggers.label: Smash or Pass internet game TikTok youth meme
triggers.description: Gaming/internet term meaning to have sex with someone or find them attractive; from Smash or Pass meme

triggers.label: Russia invasion of Ukraine German policy shift 2022
triggers.description: Referred to Chancellor Scholz's description of Russia's invasion of Ukraine as a historic turning point

triggers.label: Letzte Generation street-blockade campaign from January 2022
triggers.description: From 24 January 2022 the Letzte Generation began gluing themselves to streets in Berlin. The Bild newspaper coined 'Klimakleber' as a pejorative. By 2023 activists began using the term themselves, initiating partial reappropriation.

triggers.label: Proliferation of generative AI content
triggers.description: The public release of large generative models (ChatGPT, late 2022) and the flood of cheap machine-generated text and images that followed. By 2024 'AI slop' was an established label in technology and general press for this low-value output.

triggers.label: TikTok goofy trend German youth positive silliness
triggers.description: From English via Disney character; clumsy, silly person or behavior that amuses others; TikTok spread

triggers.label: polycrisis simultaneous Germany 2023
triggers.description: Addressed the simultaneous existence of multiple crises (climate, energy, political, economic)

triggers.label: AfD Potsdam meeting Correctiv remigration leak 2023
triggers.description: Far-right coded language for forced mass deportation of immigrants; spread via AfD and Correctiv leak

triggers.label: collapse of German traffic light coalition 2024
triggers.description: Marked the collapse of the SPD-FDP-Greens traffic light coalition under Scholz

triggers.label: TikTok gaming aura points social status youth slang 2024
triggers.description: Refers to charisma, status, and presence of a person; from gaming/TikTok 'aura points' trend
